div.p: William by the grace of God king of Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, Greeting: Let present and future know that I have granted, and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of Holyrood at Edinburgh, and to the canons serving God there, the donation that Gilbert de Umfraville made to them, of one ploughgate of land in Kinnaird, just as it was perambulated by the worthy men of the country, with common pasture as contained in the charter of the said Gilbert, and with freedom of multure and of their own men staying on their land, as contained in the charter of the said Gilbert. To be held in free and quiet and perpetual alms, as freely and quietly, fully and honourably as they hold and possess their other lands and holdings, as the charter of the said Gilbert bears witness. Reserving my service. As witnesses, Hugh my chancellor, Robert my chaplain, Jocelin the archdeacon of Dunkeld, Richard de Prebenda my clerks, Philip de Valognes, Walter de Berkeley the chamberlain, Richard son of Hugh, William de Ramsay, Adam of Ceres. At Edinburgh.
